title: Economic Policy Working and Research Papers
summary: This is a Treasury Office of Economic Policy listing page cataloging working papers from 2020, 2021, and 2024, covering topics including AI occupational exposure, mortgage rate shocks and house prices, racial labor market inequality, emergency rental assistance, and PPP loan job preservation effects. The page provides only paper titles without abstracts, full text, or publication dates for individual papers, making it insufficient for detailed macro catalyst research.
key_points: Page lists Treasury Office of Economic Policy working paper series from 2020, 2021, and 2024 with titles only, no abstracts or full text

2024 series includes papers on American Rescue Plan mapping, AI occupational exposure by geography/education, emergency rental assistance, mortgage rate shocks and house prices, and racial labor market inequality
2021 series includes papers on expectations extraction and the expectations gap
2020 series includes papers on PPP loan job preservation effects
Papers are explicitly noted as works in progress subject to revision, with views not representing official Treasury positions
No publication dates, authors, or substantive content provided for individual papers
why_it_matters: This is a Treasury Office of Economic Policy listing page cataloging working paper titles from 2020, 2021, and 2024. The page itself provides no abstracts, full text, publication dates, or substantive content. It functions as an index/catalog and is not an archiveable research document. Individual papers must be accessed separately for any meaningful macro catalyst analysis.
macro_context: The listed paper titles suggest research areas relevant to macro analysis including housing markets and mortgage rate sensitivity, labor market dynamics, AI economic impacts, and fiscal policy (ARP/PPP) effects. However, this listing page provides no substantive content to assess macro implications.
